video_title: Top 10 Tech Courses to Learn in 2025 |  Most In-Demand Tech Skills (Get Certified & Hired) | Edureka

segments.text: Have you ever glanced through job listing on LinkedIn and wondered what skills you should
develop to stay at 11th Tech?
Perhaps you are just starting off and do not want to waste your time on out-of-date courses
or random tutorials that provide no value.
Well, here in the right place.
Today, we are going into something quite practical, the top 10 trending tech courses for
These are not simply heart issues.
These are the actual courses that employees are looking for, recruiters are inquiring about
and teams are actively developing products around.
Whether you are a software engineer, a business analyst, a data geek, or just starting
out in technology.
This list will help you understand what to learn, where to acquire it and why it is
important.
But before we begin, please like, share and subscribe and don't forget to hit the bell
icon.
Also check out the Erica's website for top trending tech certification courses.
The link to which is given in the description box below.
So let's get started.
At number 10, we have no code in automation tools and underappreciated but rapidly increasing
area.
These technologies allow you to create applications, automate process and then enterprises without
ever touching code.
Consider tools like Zapier, Notion, A table, Clyde or Bubble.
Companies today demand employees who can move quickly and automate tedious tasks.
This is extremely important for operations team, digital marketers and even soul printers.
Career options include automation specialist, no good app developer and workflow designer.
Even rules suggest product operations and marketing tech specialists are adopting these
tools.
In India, the average wage for these professions is from 6 to 12 lakhs per year.
However, free Lancers with these talents often charge 1000 to 3000 per hour and platforms
like Upwork and 5-O.
It is a wonderful starting place if you do not have a coding experience but want to get
into technology.
AR and VR development ranks 9th and it's finally becoming more than a bus word.
It devices like Apple Vision Pro and MetaQa screening popularity companies in real estate,
education, gaming and even healthcare are aggressively seeking XR developers.
This course will teach you how to create a immersive experience with Unity, Unreal Engine
and tools such as AR Kit.
Career opportunities include positions such as XR developer, Unity developer and 3D simulation
engineer.
Companies that specialize in MetaQa's applications at tech or virtual training modules are constantly
seeking expertise in this field.
In India, entry-level AR VR developers earn roughly 6 LPA while professional developers
with 3D engine abilities earn 15 to 25 lakhs per year.
It is a niche with the development curve is real and rapid.
Next up, number 8 is Product Management in tech, which is suitable for those who want
to lead tech projects without having to write code.
Product managers work at the crossroads of business, design and engineering.
You'll study agile, user research, roadmap planning and how to use tools like Gira, Figma
and Confluence.
In the long run, this offers up opportunities for role-such as associate product manager,
product analyst and senior project manager.
It is also one of the best paying non-coding positions in industry.
In India, entry-level project managers can anticipate roughly 12 to 18 LPA, whereas
season PMs at huge tech organizations frequently make 30 to 60 lakhs per year, including both
bonuses and shares.
It is competitive, but if you enjoy problem solving and cross-functional work, it is an
excellent choice.
At number 7, we have Blockchain Development in Web 3.
While the cryptocurrency euphoria has subsided, practical blockchain applications are thriving.
Think about smart contracts, digital identification and asset tokenization.
These lectures often cover solidity, Ethereum, DApps and technologies such as MetaMask
integration.
Blockchain gives up opportunities for smart contract developers, Web 3 front-end engineers
and Blockchain consultants.
Companies that develop decentralized finance tools, NFT platforms or enterprise ledgers
are still actively hiding.
Blockchain developers in India get starting salary of 10 LPA with top-time professionals
earning 25 to 40 lakhs yearly, particularly with proven GitHub projects or open-source
contributions.
This is still the place to be if you want to work on cutting-edge technology.
At number 6 are DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering and Platform Engineering.
This field focuses on ensuring that software systems perform smoothly, scale efficiently
and recover quickly.
You will learn about Docker, Cubans, Jenkins and monitoring stacks like Prometheus and
Gryffana.
Most teams today seek engineers who can create code and automate infrastructure.
This course prepares you for professionals like DevOps Engineer, SRE, Platform Engineer
and even infrastructure architect.
These skills are required by all cloud-first businesses.
In India, DevOps experts with 2 to 3 years of experience typically earn 12 to 18 LPA,
whereas senior roles can come on 25 to 40 lakhs or more, particularly in FinTech, SaaS
and Global Product Forms.
If you enjoy solving problems and scaling difficulties, this is an excellent career path.
Now moving on to number 5, Cybersecurity.
As more businesses move online and manage sensitive data, the demand for cyber security
professionals has searched.
From ethical hacking to incident response, cyber security specialists are required to save
guard firms from ever-evolving threats.
Systems rely on ethical hacking and incident response against evolving threats to stay protected.
You learn about technologies such as Scali Linux, MetaSplot and Firewall management, as well
as risk assessment and security protocols.
Career options include security analyst, ethical hacker and cyber security consultant.
This area also provides a opportunity to climb to positions such as CIO.
In India, entry-level cyber security specialists can earn 6 to 12 LPA, while experience professionals
can earn 20 to 40 lakhs per year, particularly in finance, healthcare and tech companies.
With data breaches and ransomware assaults on the rise, this career path is not only profitable
but also necessary in today's digital environment.
Cloud computing and Amazon Web Services come in fourth place.
As businesses migrate from traditional data centers to the cloud, the demand for cloud
engineers, architect and developers has never been greater.
Learning AWS, Microsoft Azure or Google Cloud will provide you the ability to create and
operate scalable and secure cloud infrastructures.
You will also learn about containerization, cloud storage and DevOps integration.
Career opportunities include positions such as cloud engineers, cloud architect or cloud
solution architect.
The cloud is where the most tech companies are investing their resources.
Therefore, potential are limitless.
The average income for cloud professionals in India is approximately 10 LPA, whereas senior
architects and engineers in FinTech e-commerce and sales enterprise can earn up to 25 to 50
lakhs per year.
If you enjoy developing scalable systems and working with cutting edge infrastructure,
this is a career path worth considering.
Coming in at number three is artificial intelligence and machine learning.
AI and ML have taken the technology world by shop with applications ranging from data
analysis and self-driving cars to personalized recommendations and natural language processing.
These courses cover algorithms, deep learning and frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch and
Psychic Learning.
Data scientists, ML engineers and AI researchers are among the career options available, with
AI becoming a crucial differentiator in industries ranging from healthcare to finance.
There is a high demand for competent professionals.
In India, entry-level AI and ML specialists make 8 to 12 LPA, whereas senior professionals
earn 20 to 45 lakhs annually, particularly in AI research, computer vision or NLP
professions.
If you are interested about the future of technology and want to work at the cutting edge
of innovation, AI and machine learning are promising careers.
At number two, we have data science.
The demand for data-driven insights has never been higher, with firms from all industries
relying on data scientists to make educated decisions and a chief business outcomes.
This course will teach you how to clean data, analyze statistics, visualize data and use
machine learning.
You will also gain hands-on expertise with art, Python, SQL and data visualization technologies
such as tabloon and power BI.
Career opportunities include professionals such as data analyst, data scientist and data
engineer.
This is one of the most lucrative professions in technology right now.
With numerous chances and industries such as e-commerce, banking, health care and others.
In India, entry-level data scientists earn between 8 to 15 LPA, but experience profession
might come on 25 to 50 lakhs or more, especially in digital startups and worldwide organizations.
If you enjoy working with numbers and are passionate about extra-ten significance insights, data
science is an excellent career path.
And finally, at the top of the list is software development, specifically full stack, front-end
and back-end development.
This is the foundation of the tech industry, whether you are developing website, mobile
app or big business systems.
You will be working with programming languages such as JavaScript, Python and Java, as well
as frameworks like React, Angular and Node.js.
Full stack web development in particular is one of the most sought after skills, because
it encompasses both front-end and back-end programming.
Career and software development are diverse and profitable.
You could work as a front-end developer, back-end developer, full stack developer or
a software engineer.
This industry provides excellent job stability and growth opportunity.
Entry-level developers in India often earn 6 to 12 LPA, while senior developers might
earn 20 to 50 Lakhs annually, depending on the organization and experience.
Big IT organizations and worldwide startups are looking for top-tier talent.
And if you enjoy coding, software development remains one of the finest job options for
2020-25 and later.
And that's it, whether you want to upskill, transition into technology or go further into
areas of expertise.
These courses can lead to a variety of exciting and well-paying employment.
The technology sector is changing faster than ever, and with appropriate course, you can
be a part of it.
So which course is drawing your attention?
Please let me know in the comments below.
If you've enjoyed listening to this video, please be kind enough to like it and you
can comment on any of your doubts and queries.
We will reply to them as soon as possible.
Look for more videos and playlist and subscribe to EditingCars YouTube channel to learn more.
Happy learning!
